observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to
instruct the (a) City Treasurer to send written notice to the payee of
the existence of the check at least one month before a check becomes
stale; and (b) Acting City Accountant to take up the necessary
adjustment for the determined stale check in the books of account.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) Acting City
Accountant and City Treasurer to reconcile their records of RPT and
SET Receivables in order to report reliably the said accounts in the
financial statements; (b) City Assessor to submit the monthly
assessment roll as well as other reports needed to update the Certified
List of RPT Delinquencies to the City Treasurer; and (c) City Treasurer
to strictly enforce the remedies for the collection of delinquent RPT as
expressly provided in Section 256 of R.A. No. 7160 and institute
appropriate strategies to further improve the collection efficiency.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and the LCE agreed
to direct (a) all accountable officers and employees to ensure
liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period as required
by COA Circular No. 97-002; (b) the Acting City Accountant, in
collaboration with the City Legal Officer, to initiate procedures in the
filing of request for write-off of dormant unliquidated cash advances
if the conditions and circumstances prescribed under COA Circular
No. 2016-005 on writing off the accounts are met; (c) the Budget
Officer, Acting City Accountant, and City Treasurer to refrain from
granting additional cash advance to any official or employee unless
the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or properly
accounted for; (d) the City Treasurer to consider increasing the
amount of Fidelity Bond of the Disbursing Officer whose cash
advances exceeded his maximum cash accountability, as provided
in Annex C of the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009, or to
designate additional Disbursing Officer/(s); and (e) the Acting City
Accountant to immediately submit to the Audit Team the noted
liquidation reports on cash advances totaling ₱1,143,400.00 for
audit and verification.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) Acting City
Accountant to (i) conduct verification, analysis and validation of the
existence of dormant inventory account amounting to ₱574,605.59; (ii)
secure/collate all available documents relative to the said account; (iii)
refer to Item 7.0 of COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023
on the procedures for the proper disposition of dormant inventory
account; and (iv) maintain SLC for all inventory accounts; and (b)
CGSO to (i) conduct physical count of supplies and materials
semestrally and to report the same in the RPCI for submission to the
Audit Team not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year; and
(ii) maintain SC for each type of supplies to account for the receipt,
issuance, and balance of the same at any given period.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and the LCE agreed
to require the (a) Inventory Committee and GSO to (i) conduct a
complete physical count of all PPEs of the City, conclude said
inventory count for CY 2024 and immediately submit the corresponding
Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) to Audit Team; and
(ii) reconcile the resultant inventory report with the accounting
records; (b) City Accounting Office (CAO) and City General Services
Office (CGSO) to maintain complete property records for each category
of PPE; and (c) Acting City Accountant and CGSO to consider the one-
time cleansing of PPE accounts following the guidelines under COA
Circular No. 2020-006, to improve the reliability of the PPE accounts.

We also recommended that the Disposal Committee facilitate the
immediate disposal of unserviceable motor vehicles costing
₱23,704,629.62 in accordance with the prescribed guidelines and
procedures.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Acting City
Accountant to continue complying with Section 50 of the NGAS
Manual for LGUs, Volume I on (i) classifying and recording PPE as
CIP during the construction period and (ii) reclassifying completed
projects to appropriate PPE accounts.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the City Planning
& Development Officer (CPDO), or the official responsible in the
formulation of PPAs to coordinate with the Local BFP for the
identification of PPAs where the unused funds could be applied.

We also reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LCE
direct the (a) Acting City Accountant and the implementing offices to
cause the immediate liquidation and return of the unutilized funds to
the respective source agencies or the Bureau of Treasury in
observance of COA Circular No. 94-013; and (b) Acting City
Accountant, City Legal Officer and Acting City Treasurer to review the
dormant accounts under the trust funds, retrieve their MOA/SARO and
other relevant documents, and determine whether: (i) the unutilized fund
balances could be legitimately used by the City despite the passage of
unreasonable time or seek authority from the donor or sponsor on the
possibility of utilizing the same for the purpose other than those
provided in the MOA/SARO; and (ii) make proper liquidation and
reporting of the unexpended balances of the trusts to the respective
donor/sponsor agencies.

In view of the above audit observations, we recommended and the
LCE agreed to: (a) prioritize the computation, funding and payment
of unpaid terminal leave benefits of employees who were separated
from government service to comply with RA No. 10154; (b) recognize
and make necessary adjustments in the Employee Benefits account,
update/reconcile employee leave credits and perform other relevant
activities within three years from the effectivity of COA Circular No.
2024-009; and (c) ensure that the disbursement vouchers are
supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4
(6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14,
2012 and submit the lacking documents enumerated in Appendix C.

We recommended and the LCE agreed, through the Acting City
Accountant, to ensure (a) full compliance with the disclosure
requirements provided under IPSAS 3; and (b) that current year
expenses be recorded in the current period, rather than adjusted
against prior periods to observe proper matching of expenses against
appropriation, pursuant to Section 119 of PD No. 1445 and Section
350 of RA No. 7160.

We reiterated our recommendation and the LCE agreed to require the
(a) coordination of the CPLO, City Treasurer and Sangguniang
Panlungsod to (i) regularly review and update the City Revenue Code
at least every five years, as mandated by law, in order to optimize its
revenue generation for the benefit of its constituents; and (ii) ensure
that the amended Revenue Code reflects the current economic
condition and complies with the Local Government Code of 1991; (b)
CPLO to (i) be more strict in the implementation and enforcement of
the City Revenue Code; (ii) secure from the Accounting Office any
report that can be useful in the assessment of business tax for business
entities that have transactions with the City; and (iii) require the
business tax payers to submit copies of the respective ITRs for the
proper computation of deficiency taxes, if any; and (c) CPLO and City
Treasurer to verify, assess, and validate other businesses aside from
the noted business taxpayers and demand the remittance of computed
under collection of business taxes amounting to ₱147,445.14.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) LDC through
the City Planning and Development Officer to (i) strengthen the
planning process; and (ii) ensure the viability of all planned projects
to achieve full implementation and maximize fund utilization; and (b)
Bids and Awards Committee, BAC Secretariat, and Technical
Working Group to strictly comply with pertinent provisions of R.A.
No. 9184 and its IRR in all procurements.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) OIC-City
Human Resources Management Officer (OIC-CHRMO) to (i)
consider prioritizing the filling-up of open regular vacant positions;
and (ii) be prudent in the hiring of Job Orders (JOs) and Contract of
Service (COS) workers that could result in unnecessary workforce
such as those that duplicate the works performed by regular
personnel; (b) OIC-CHRMO and Acting City Accountant to (i) submit
regularly to the Audit Team the copies of Contracts of JOs/COS
personnel; and (ii) ensure that Accomplishment Reports of JO/COS
workers are submitted as part of documentary requirements to
support their claims.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and the LCE
agreed to direct the (a) CDRRMC to: (i) ensure the optimal utilization
of LDRRMF through a more realistic planning, strengthened
implementation, and effective monitoring of all DRRM programs,
projects and activities for the year; (ii) identify and incorporate under
a separate caption in the LDRRMFIP, the projects and activities to be
charged out of the unexpended LDRRMF from previous years to
facilitate monitoring and evaluation of the activities of the fund; (b)
BAC and Acting City Accountant to submit all the requisite
documents on the audited disbursements totaling ₱11,996,697.74, and
henceforth ensure full compliance with R.A. No. 9184 and COA
Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012; and (c) concerned offices
to continue complying with RA No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-
002 dated September 12, 2012 with the end view of building disaster-
resilient communities; and (d) OIC – CDRRMO to explain the non-
implementation of 38 PPAs.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and the LCE agreed
to direct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), through the GAD Focal
Person to (a) continue its efforts to improve gender mainstreaming in
achieving full compliance with the provisions of relevant laws and
regulations pertaining to GAD; (b) employ the HGDG in the
attribution of costs of regular program/project to GAD; (c) ensure the
complete implementation of activities indicated in the GPB to
maximize the intervention in addressing gender-issues in the City; and
(d) nominate the GFPS members for seminars/trainings on gender
analysis to familiarize themselves with the process and employment of
HGDG in the attribution of costs of regular program/project to GAD.

We further recommended that the GFPS, through the GAD Focal
Person, inform the concerned DILG Office, in writing, of changes
introduced in the DILG-endorsed GPB and to prepare and submit
revised GAD Accomplishment Report whenever necessary.

In view of the above audit observations, we recommended and the
Management agreed to: (a) strictly adhere to the aforesaid provisions
on the preparation and utilization of SEF Budget; (b) ensure that the
disbursement vouchers are supported with complete documentation in
compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No.
2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 (and submit the lacking documents as
enumerated in Annex A of this AOM); (c) discontinue the allocation of
funds for expenses that are not among those prioritized to be charged
against the SEF; (d) instruct the Engineering Office to administer,
supervise and monitor engineering works for all infrastructures of the
City; and (f) discontinue the practice of charging prior year’s expenses
against current year’s appropriation to ensure proper matching of
expenses against appropriation for a particular year.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the City Treasurer to
continue to require all RCOs to report daily the collections by preparing
the RCD at the close of each business day and remit the same intact to
him for deposit to the authorized government depository bank.

We recommended that the Acting City Accountant and Acting City
Treasurer continue to comply with the BIR rules and regulations on
the proper withholding and remittance of taxes on compensation,
value added taxes, percentage taxes and expanded taxes from
government money payments.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and the LCE agreed
to direct the (a) Acting City Accountant to continue the reconciliation
between her records and the billing from the GSIS for her to settle
immediately the established variance/difference which appeared as the
City’s unremitted trust liabilities; and (b) Acting City Accountant and
Acting City Treasurer to further improve their compliance on GSIS
rules and regulations by having close supervision for the immediate
disposition of variance/difference between the monthly withheld GSIS
contributions and the corresponding remittances.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Acting City
Accountant and Acting City Treasurer continue complying with the
provisions of R.A. No. 9679 or HDMF law of 2009 on the withholding
of contributions and loan amortizations from employees’
compensation together with the government’s share and its timely
remittances to HDMF.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Acting City
Accountant and Acting City Treasurer continue complying with the
R.A. No. 7875 as amended by R.A. Nos. 9241 and 10606 or the
National Health Insurance Act of 2013 on the withholding of
contributions from employees’ compensation together with the
government’s share and its timely remittances to PHIC.

We recommended that the LCE direct the (a) accountable officials and
employees of the City to exercise due prudence in the disbursement of
government funds to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances; and (b)
Acting City Accountant, in coordination with City Legal Office, to (i)
exhaust all possible means for the collection/recovery of the disallowed
transactions except the one on appeal from the persons liable
therefrom; and (ii) require the persons responsible for the immediate
settlement of the balances of audit suspensions through cash payments
or submission of lacking documents within the prescribed period
before they mature into audit disallowances.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the City Treasurer/GSO to prioritize the registration of all motor vehicles owned by the City with the LTO to prevent possible loss and misuse of the assets and to avoid the incurrence of possible penalties, fines or sanctions from the LTO.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the CGSO to facilitate the insurance of all motor vehicles owned by the City so that the LGU may be indemnified or compensated for any damage or loss due to possible casualties that may happen on these insurable assets.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Acting City Accountant to continue complying with Section 50 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I on (i) classifying and recording PPE as CIP during the construction period and (ii) reclassifying completed projects to appropriate PPE accounts.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the City Planning & Development Officer (CPDO), or the official responsible in the formulation of PPAs to coordinate with the Local BFP for the identification of PPAs where the unused funds could be applied. We also reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and the LCE agreed to direct the Acting City Accountant and the Acting City Treasurer to cause the immediate return of the unutilized funds to the respective Source Agencies and the Bureau of Treasury in observance of COA Circular No. 94-013; and (b) Acting City Accountant, City Legal Officer and Acting City Treasurer to review the dormant accounts under the trust funds, retrieve their MOA/SARO and other relevant documents, and determine whether: (i) the unutilized fund balances could be legitimately used by the City despite the passage of unreasonable time or seek authority from the donor or sponsor on the possibility of utilizing the same for the purpose other than those provided in the MOA/SARO; and (ii) make proper liquidation and reporting of the unexpended balances of the trusts to the respective donors/sponsors.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and  the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the (a) Acting City Accountant and City Treasurer to reconcile their records of RPT and SET Receivables in order to report reliably the said accounts in the financial statements;  (b) City Assessor to submit the monthly assessment roll as well as other reports needed to update the Certified List of RPT Delinquencies to Acting City Treasurer; and (c) City Treasurer to strictly enforce the remedies for the collection of delinquent RPT as expressly provided in Section 256 of R.A. No. 7160 and institute appropriate strategies to further improve the collection efficiency.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the City Treasurer/GSO to prioritize the registration of all motor vehicles owned by the City with the LTO to prevent possible loss and misuse of the assets and to avoid the incurrence of possible penalties, fines or sanctions from the LTO.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the CGSO to facilitate the insurance of all motor vehicles owned by the City so that the LGU may be indemnified or compensated for any damage or loss due to possible casualties that may happen on these insurable assets.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Acting City Accountant to continue complying with Section 50 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I on (i) classifying and recording PPE as CIP during the construction period and (ii) reclassifying completed projects to appropriate PPE accounts.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the City Planning & Development Officer (CPDO), or the official responsible in the formulation of PPAs to coordinate with the Local BFP for the identification of PPAs where the unused funds could be applied.
We also reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) Acting City Accountant and the Acting City Treasurer to cause the immediate return of the unutilized funds to the respective source agencies and the Bureau of Treasury in observance of COA Circular No. 94-013; and (b) Acting City Accountant, City Legal Officer and Acting City Treasurer to review the dormant accounts under the trust funds, retrieve their MOA/SARO and other relevant documents, and determine whether: (i) the unutilized fund balances could be legitimately used by the City despite the passage of unreasonable time or seek authority from the donor or sponsor on the possibility of utilizing the same for the purpose other than those provided in the MOA/SARO; and (ii) make proper liquidation and reporting of the unexpended balances of the trusts to the respective donors/sponsors.
Additionally, we recommended that Management strictly comply with pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR in all procurements; and instruct the Acting City Accountant to strengthen her review function on the requisite documentation of procurements.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct (a) the Acting City Accountant to (i) perform verification and reconciliation of balances of Due to LGUs account of the City with the Due from LGUs account of the Provincial Government to establish the correct and accurate balances of both accounts; (ii) draw the necessary journal entries to reflect the corrected/adjusted balances of Due to LGUs; and (b) the Acting City Treasurer and Acting City Accountant to initiate the regular/timely release of the quarterly RPT shares for all the Barangays of the City pursuant to Section 271(d) of R.A. 7160.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) LDC through the City Planning and Development Officer to (i) strengthen the planning process; (ii) ensure the viability of all planned projects to maximize fund utilization; and (iii) adhere to the requirements of Item 3.2.5 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in the utilization of the 20% DF, henceforth sustain the promotion of the objectives of the said fund by ensuring that only eligible charges are made against it; (b) Bids and Awards Committee, BAC Secretariat, and Technical Working Group to (i) submit copies of Performance Security of the winning bidders for the two infrastructure projects; otherwise, provide satisfactory explanation of the non-submission thereof; and (ii) strictly comply with pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR in all procurements;  and (c) Acting City Accountant to strengthen her review function on the requisite documentation of procurements.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) CDRRMC to:  (i) ensure the optimal utilization of LDRRMF through a more realistic project planning and budgeting; (ii) identify and incorporate, under a separate caption, in the LDRRMFIP the projects and activities to be charged out of the unexpended LDRRMF from previous years to facilitate monitoring and evaluation of the activities of the fund; and (b) BAC and Acting City Accountant to submit all the requisite documents, and henceforth ensure full compliance with R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012; and (c) concerned offices to continue complying with RA No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 with the end view of building disaster-resilient communities.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and the LCE agreed to direct (a) all accountable officers and employees to ensure liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period as required by COA Circular No. 97-002; (b) the Acting City Accountant, in collaboration with the City Legal Officer, to initiate procedures in the filing of request for write-off of dormant unliquidated cash advances if the conditions and circumstances prescribed under COA Circular 2016-005 on writing off the accounts are met; (c) the Budget Officer, Acting City Accountant, and Acting City Treasurer to refrain from granting additional cash advance to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him is  first settled or properly accounted for; (d) the Acting City Treasurer to consider increasing the amount of  Fidelity Bond of the Disbursing Officer whose cash advances exceeded his maximum cash accountability, as provided in Annex C of the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009, or to designate additional Disbursing Officer/(s); and (e) the Acting City Accountant to expedite the submission to the Audit Team of the liquidation reports on cash advances totaling ₱54,029,300.00 for audit and verification.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the City Treasurer to continue to require all RCOs to report daily the collections by preparing the RCD at the close of each business day and remit the same intact to him for deposit to the authorized government depository bank.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a) coordination of the CPLO, City Treasurer and Sangguniang Panglungsod to (i) regularly review and update the City Revenue Code at least every five years, as mandated by law, in order to optimize its revenue generation for the benefit of its constituents; (ii) ensure that the amended Revenue Code reflects the current economic conditions and complies with the Local Government Code of 1991; (b) CPLO to (i) be more strict in the implementation and enforcement of the City Revenue Code; (ii) secure from the Accounting Office any reports that can be useful in the assessment of business tax for business entities that have transaction with the City; and (iii) require the business tax payers to submit a copy of ITR for the proper computation of deficiency tax, if any; and (c) CPLO and City Treasurer to verify, assess, and validate other businesses aside from the noted business taxpayer and demand the remittance of computed under collection of business taxes amounting to ₱135,933.80.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the: (a) OIC-CENRO, CPDO and CBO to ensure the optimal implementation of SWM PPAs through a more realistic project planning and budgeting; and (b) CBO and Acting City Accountant to discontinue the practice of charging prior year’s expenses against current year’s appropriation and that proper matching of expenses against appropriation should always be observed, pursuant to Section 119 of PD 1445 and Sections 305 & 350 of RA No. 7160.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and the LCE agreed to direct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), through the GAD Focal Person to (a) continue its efforts to improve gender mainstreaming to achieve full compliance with the provisions of relevant laws and regulations; (b) employ the HGDG in the attribution of costs of regular program/project to GAD; (c) ensure the complete implementation of activities indicated in the GPB to maximize the intervention in addressing gender-issues in the City; and (d) nominate the GFPS members for  seminars/trainings on gender analysis to familiarize themselves with the process and employment of HGDG in the attribution of costs of regular program/project to GAD.
We further recommended that the GFPS, through the GAD Focal Person, inform the concerned DILG Office, in writing, of changes introduced in the DILG-endorsed GPB and to prepare and submit revise GAD Accomplishment Report whenever necessary.

We recommended that the Acting City Accountant and Acting City Treasurer continue to comply with the BIR rules and regulations on the withholding and remittance of taxes on compensation, value added taxes, percentage taxes and expanded taxes from government money payments.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) Acting City Accountant to continue the reconciliation between her records and the billing from GSIS for her to settle immediately the established variance/difference which appears to be as City’s unremitted trust liabilities; and (b) Acting City Accountant and Acting City Treasurer to further improve their compliance on GSIS rules and regulations by having close supervision for the immediate disposition of variance/difference between the monthly withheld GSIS contributions and the corresponding remittances.

We recommended that the Acting City Accountant and Acting City Treasurer to continue complying with the R.A. No. 9679 or HDMF law of 2009 on the withholding of contributions and loan amortizations from employees’ compensation together with the government’s share and its timely remittances to HDMF.

We recommended that the Acting City Accountant and Acting City Treasurer continue complying with the R.A. No. 7875 as amended by R.A. Nos. 9241 and 10606 or the National Health Insurance Act of 2013 on the withholding of contributions from employees’ compensation together with the government’s share and its timely remittances to PHIC.

We recommended that the LCE direct the (a) accountable officials and employees of the City to exercise due prudence in the disbursement of government funds to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances; and (b) Acting City Accountant, in coordination with City Legal Office, to (i) exhaust all possible means for the collection/recovery of the disallowed transactions except the one on appeal from the persons liable therefrom; and (ii) require the persons responsible for the immediate settlement of the balances of audit suspensions through cash payments or submission of lacking documents within the prescribed period before they mature into audit disallowances.

We recommended that the LCE require the Acting City Engineer, CSWD Officer and Heads of the Offices concerned to strictly observe the time frame of scheduled implementation/ completion of the projects to ensure efficient delivery of public services to the intended beneficiaries.  

We also reiterate our prior year’s recommendation that the 

(a) Acting City Accountant and the Acting City Treasurer to cause the immediate return of the unutilized funds to the respective source agencies and Bureau of Treasury in observance of COA Circular No. 94-013; and 

(b) Acting City Accountant, City Legal Officer and Acting City Treasurer to review all dormant accounts under the trust funds, retrieve their MOA/SARO and other relevant documents, and determine whether: (i) the unutilized fund balances could be legitimately used by the City despite the passage of unreasonable time or seek authority from the donor or sponsor on the possibility of utilizing the same for the purpose other than those provided in the MOA/SARO; and 


(ii) make proper liquidations and reporting of the unexpended balance of the trusts to respective donors/sponsors.
observations.managementAction:
The cause of the delay is the transition of local chief executive and the changes in Program of Works.




All dormant accounts under the trust funds were carried over from prior years’ balances, the details of which could no longer be determined due to unavailability of records and ledgers to support. OCA will exert extra effort to retrieve MOA/SARO and other relevant documents.

Proper liquidations and report of unutilized funds were already made and returned to the respective donors the unexpended balance.

observations.recommendation:
1.16 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the (a) Acting
City Treasurer to (i) employ effective collection strategies by updating and posting
the notice of delinquency for property taxes at designated areas and conduct massive
campaign and public information dissemination on the value of paying taxes; and if
warranted, enforce the provision of Section 254(b) of the RA 7160 in the collection of
delinquent taxes; and (ii) timely submit to Accounting Office a Certified List of
Taxpayers and Taxes Due as the basis in setting up of RPT and SET receivables in
the books of accounts of the City at the beginning of the year; and (b) Acting City
Accountant and Acting City Treasurer to reconcile their records of RPT and SET
receivables in order to report reliably the said accounts in the financial statements.

2.33 We recommended that management organize a Market Committee composed
of representatives from various Offices to perform the duties affecting market
operations enumerated in the Market Code. We also recommended that the LCE
direct the (a) Acting City Engineer to prepare an updated or reconstruct the actual
structure lay out plan of the main building and wet section of public market; and (b)
Acting City Treasurer and the designated Market Administrator (i) to issue the
necessary follow up/demand letters to the delinquent stall owners/occupants for the
settlement of their over-due obligations or initiate legal action, if warranted, to collect
said arrearages; (iii) require the stall owners to observe faithful compliance of their
duties and obligations as stated in the contract and penalize them in case of any
breach or violation stipulated thereto, particularly on the prohibited sub-leasing of
stalls ; (iv) submit an updated master list of stall owners and reconcile the same with
the actual occupants and numbers of market stalls appearing in the updated CEO’s
structure layout plan; (v) ensure that tenants are charged with prescribed rental fees
inclusive of the escalation; (vi) enforce revocation or cancellation of lease of stalls
closed for thirty (30) consecutive days and conduct publication of offering to avoid
idleness; and (vi) exert effort to locate the copies of contract of 248 stall owners,
otherwise require them to execute updated and valid contracts.

3.19 We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LCE require the (a)
Inventory Committee and CGSO Head to (i) conduct physical inventory of all PPE of
the City on or before January 31 of each year, conclude said inventory count for CY
2022 and immediately submit the related reports to the Audit Team; and (ii) reconcile
the resultant inventory reports with the accounting records; and (b) Acting City
Accountant and CGSO Head to (i) maintain complete PPE records such as PPELCs
and PCs for the proper control and monitoring of the agency property and equipment
and (ii) consider the guidelines on one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to render a
reliable PPE information.
3.20 We further recommended that the LCE direct the (a) Officer-In-Charge (OIC)
- CDRRMO to (i) prepare an assessment report of the estimated amounts of damages
in the properties and equipment of the control command center; and (ii) furnish the
Acting City Accountant of the said report as basis in the recording of Impairment Loss
on PPE.

4.6 We recommended that the Acting City Treasurer require all RCOs to report
daily the collections by preparing the RCD at the close of each business day and remit
the same intact to him for deposit to the authorized government depository bank.

5.24 We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LCE direct (a) all
accountable officers and employees to ensure liquidation of cash advances within the
prescribed period as required by COA Circulars No. 97-002; and (b) the Acting City
Accountant, in collaboration with the City Legal Officer, to initiate procedures for
the application of the request for write-off if the conditions and circumstances
prescribed under COA Circular 2016-005 on writing off the accounts are present.
5.25 We also recommended that the (a) management refrain from granting
additional cash advances to officers and employees unless the previous cash advance
given to them is first settled; and (b) LCE direct the (i) Acting City Treasurer to
consider increasing the amount of the Fidelity Bond of the disbursing officer whose
cash advances exceeded his maximum cash accountability as required under Annex
C of the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 or to designate additional
disbursing officer/(s); and (ii) the Acting City Accountant expedite the submission of
unsubmitted liquidation reports for audit and verification.

6.8 We recommended that the LCE require the Acting City Treasurer and Acting
City Accountant to initiate the regular/timely release of the quarterly RPT shares for
all the Barangays of the City pursuant to Section 271(d) of R.A. 7160.

7.18 We recommended that the LCE require the Acting City Engineer, CSWD
Officer and Heads of the Offices concerned to strictly observe the time frame of
scheduled implementation/completion of the projects to ensure efficient delivery of
public services to the intended beneficiaries. We also reiterate our prior year’s
recommendation that the (a) Acting City Accountant and the Acting City Treasurer
to cause the immediate return of the unutilized funds to the respective source agencies
and Bureau of Treasury in observance of COA Circular No. 94-013; and (b) Acting
City Accountant, City Legal Officer and Acting City Treasurer to review all dormant
accounts under the trust funds, retrieve their MOA/SARO and other relevant
documents, and determine whether: (i) the unutilized fund balances could be
legitimately used by the City despite the passage of unreasonable time or seek
authority from the donor or sponsor on the possibility of utilizing the same for the
purpose other than those provided in the MOA/SARO; and (ii) make proper
liquidations and reporting to respective donors/sponsors for unexpended balance of
the trusts.

8.12 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Acting City Engineer, City
Planning and Development Officer and other concerned officials, to thoroughly study
all feasible solutions in order to swiftly and successfully implement the construction
of City Hospital and other priority projects for the immediate benefits of the
constituents. We also reiterate our prior year’s recommendation that the LCE (a)
ensure the full or optimum utilization of the 20% Development Fund during the
budget year; and (b) require the concerned officers to be prudent in overseeing the
timely implementation of PPAs under their responsibility.

9.8 We recommended that the City Development Council headed by the LCE
continue its adherence to Section 3.2.5 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 and in
implementing development projects that are essential in the promotion of the general
welfare of the City’s constituents.

10.10 We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LCE require the (a)
Bids and Awards Committee; BAC Secretariat, and Technical Working Group
(TWG) to ensure compliance with the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its
IRR in all procurements through public bidding; and (b) Acting City Accountant to
strengthen her review function on the requisite documentation of procurements. We
also recommended that the LCE direct the Acting City Engineer to submit copies of
Performance Security of the winning bidders for the three infrastructure projects;
otherwise, provide satisfactory explanation on the non-submission thereof.

11.28 We recommended that the LCE direct the (a) OIC-CDRRMO, in
collaboration with LDRRMC, to (i) identify and incorporate, under a separate
caption, in the LDRRMFIP the projects and activities to be charged out of the
unexpended LDRRMF from previous years to facilitate monitoring and evaluation of
the activities of the fund; (ii) properly and effectively identify LDRRMF related PPAs
to be undertaken in order to ensure their full implementation and attainment of the
expected outcomes; and (iii) update or revise the LDRRMFIP as necessary to effect
the changes in the PPAs to be implemented; (b) Chairman, BAC and Head, BAC
Secretariat to (i) ensure that the necessary procurement information or disclosures
are timely posted in the PhilGEPS and conspicuous places; and (ii) submit the noted
deficiencies in the procurement process and/or transmit the absent or lacking
documents as discussed above; (c) OIC-CDRRMO and CGSO Head to submit the
distribution list/list of recipients of the relief goods for the evaluation of Audit Team;
and (d) Acting City Accountant to (i) strengthen her review function on the requisite
documentation of procurements; and (ii) expedite the submission of still
untransmitted DVs including the required documentary requirements.

12.11 We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LCE direct the
CENRO to prioritize and pursue the establishment and operation of the City’s final
disposal facility and to implement strategies mentioned in the Ten-Year SWMP in
order to minimize the payment of tipping fees. We further recommended that the
LCE instruct the Barangay Officials, in coordination with CENRO, to consider
inclusion of composting operation in the MRFs and ensure that they are fully
operating and functioning pursuant to Section 1, Rule XI of the IRR of RA No. 9003.

13.8 We recommended that the LCE direct the Acting City Engineer, CPDO and
all concerned Department Heads to (a) submit to the Audit Team at the beginning of
the year the list of all on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during
the year; and (b) ensure that the appropriate signboard containing the information
of the project is displayed in conspicuous places within the City premises, and in the
venue where the PPA is located or carried out as required by Item 2.2.1 of COA
Circular No. 2013-004.

14.21 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) HRMO III to (i) submit
regularly to the Audit Team the copies of Contracts for JO and COS workers within
the prescribed period as required by COA Circular No. 2009-001; and (ii) implement
control measures on the complete and timely submission of DTRs and ARs of JO/COS
Personnel; (b) Acting City Accountant ensure completeness of supporting documents
to substantiate the payroll claims; and (c) Acting City Treasurer to order the
Disbursing Officer to release the wages directly to the claimants/payees, otherwise
require the submission of authorization together with valid identification cards of
both payees and representative and attached the same in the paid payrolls.

15.10 We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive direct the Acting City Treasurer and Acting City Accountant to (a) exert extra
efforts and plan out efficient ways to reduce, if not completely eliminate, the
continuous incurrence of delays in the submission of accounts.; and (b) cause the
immediate submission of untransmitted CY 2022 accounts, records and reports to
the Audit Team.

16.19 We recommended that the LCE direct the BAC Chairman, BAC Secretariat
and City Health Officer to submit the lacking requirements in the procurement of
ancillary supplies and goods and services as required by GPPB Circular No. 02-2021.
We also recommend that the management continue its efforts to promote and

observations.recommendation:
We therefore reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:
City Treasurer to (i) prepare an updated Certified List of Taxpayers, bearing the names, amount due and collectible during the year, and submit the same to the accounting unit as the basis in setting up of RPT/SET receivable in the succeeding year; (ii) ensure that the list of taxpayers bear vital information, such as the addresses, to facilitate, if necessary, the sending of notices and other follow up communications relative to tax campaign activities and other collection strategy that may be  implemented by Management; and (iii) introduce collection strategy that would significantly help increase the tax collection of the City.
City Accountant to (i) observe the provision under Section 20 of the New Government Accounting (NGAS) Manual; and (ii) use the Certified List of Taxpayers and tax due prepared by the Office of the City Treasurer as the basis in recording the RPT/SET receivables at the beginning of the year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC City Accountant to:
immediately cause the issuance of the necessary Demand Letters to the persons responsible/liable for the liquidation of the ₱ 9,782,295.49 cash advances;
ensure submission of the liquidation reports and/or settlement of the same to the Office of the COA Auditor for review. The necessary complete documents must be attached to the liquidation reports, such as the original official receipts, certificate of seminar/trainings completed, etc.; and
introduce controls that would assure that cash advances are liquidated or reported as soon as the purpose of the cash advance is served to avoid the recurrence of similar lapses, in compliance with Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular 97-002.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive (a) direct the Office of the City Treasurer and the Office of the Market Supervisor to issue follow up demand letters for the settlement of their delinquent accounts; (b) initiate legal action, if warranted, to collect the long overdue arrearages; and (c) strictly enforce the applicable provisions of the contract/agreement pertaining to the obligation of the stall occupants in order to prevent the recurrence of similar problem in the future.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
instruct the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to cause the immediate submission of report of disbursements and to return  the unutilized funds to the Bureau of Soils and Water Management and the Department of Agriculture Regional Office III the amount of ₱86,928.00 and ₱2,970.66 respectively; and
require the respective Head of the Offices concerned to strictly observe the time frame of scheduled implementation of the projects to avoid the recurrence of similar lapses in the future and in order to provide efficient delivery of public services for the benefits of the constituents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) continue to invest in high-impact capital expenditures projects in the utilization of its 20% Development Fund  and refrain from using the fund to  non-capital investment projects; (b) ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the proposed projects within their targeted completion period through close monitoring and supervision to maximize the developmental benefits and the early enjoyment thereof by the intended beneficiaries; and (c) instruct the City Planning and Development Officer, the Sanggunian and the City Budget Officer to (i) refrain from appropriating/allocating funds for programs/projects/activities (PPAs) under the 20% Development Fund which are not capital in nature; and (ii) ensure that the Fund is only utilized for its intended purpose.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the CRRDMC to revisit and constantly monitor the status of implementation of approved plans within the specified time frame. Further, timely utilize the CDRRM Funds for PPAs that would prepare, mitigate and reduce the impact and/ or effect of calamities and adversities that may occur in the area.
Furthermore, initiate actions that would help CDRRM Office improve its services by providing the necessary training and conducting simulation exercises with members of the Emergency Response Teams
We reiterated our recommendation that the City Treasurer cause the immediate return of the unutilized PDAF funds to the National Treasury.

We recommended that the Local School Board (a) revisit its Annual Investment Plan and give priority consideration in the appropriation of budget on prescribed priority programs/projects/activities which will give more desirable benefits for the students; (b) prepare the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and submit the same to the Office of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) for the timely and systematic scheduling of the procurement activities under SEF ; (c) observe full compliance with the guidelines on complete documentation of every transaction; (d) monitor the status of availability of sufficient fund in order to avoid unauthorized use of SEF and incurrence of overdraft; and (e) observe the procedures on the issuance of the necessary Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment (ARE) corresponding to the property issued to the DepEd DO custodian for proper and easy determination of accountability over the said government property.

We recommended and Management agreed that the activities or duties performed by the officials of NEUST in addition to, or over and above their regular functions be enumerated so as to determine the basis of the grant of the honoraria.
We also recommended that the said honoraria be remitted to NEUST who shall cause the payment of the same to the concerned officials.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the City Budget Officer for the inclusion in the next budget preparation the appropriation of sufficient amount for the insurance coverage of all insurable properties of the City; and (b) remind the General Services Officer to coordinate with the City Accountant and the City Treasurer for the timely processing and payment of GSIS insurance for insurable PPEs in compliance with RA 656.

We recommended that the (a) City Planning Officer, the Health Officer and other concerned City officials to immediately coordinate with the barangay officials concerned  for the strict implementation of the City Ecological Solid Waste Management Ordinance of 2012, particularly on the prohibition on the littering, dumping and open burning of solid wastes like hay stalks, dried leaves and other farm wastes; (b) The City Agriculturist encourage farmers to do composting of farm waste to enrich the soil with natural fertilizers; (c) City Environment Officer together with the City Health Officer conduct proper information dissemination like conducting seminars, production of posters/tarpaulins on the harmful effects to the health of the public of smoke from burning of solid wastes; and (d) CENRO officials assert their authority in the implementation of the ordinance prohibiting the burning of hay stalks and other farm wastes.

We recommended that the members of the SP and PUTFRB consider enacting appropriate restrictions and monitoring requirements in the ordinance on the regulation of the operations of three-wheeled public conveyance to prevent undesirable outcomes as a result of unregulated operation of tricycles along the national highways within the City, which may further pose hazards and imminent danger to pedestrians and motorists’ lives and properties.

We recommended that Management require the City Human Resource Management Office (CHRMO) and the GAD Focal Point Person to (a) complete the GAD Database; (b) prepare LGU Status Report on Institutional Mechanisms for Gender Mainstreaming; and (c) closely monitor the establishment of Barangay GFPS in all barangays in order to ensure and sustain the LGUs critical consciousness in supporting gender and development, women’s empowerment and responding to gender issues as prescribed in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
Further, we recommended that Management submit the Annual GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) duly approved by the PCW and/ or DILG Regional Office. Furthermore, strictly adhere in the preparation and timely submission of the corresponding accomplishment reports to COA Audit Team pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive remind the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to observe strict compliance with GSIS rules and regulations and introduce measures that will enable them to monitor the monthly remittance status of GSIS contributions withheld by the City Government.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to prioritize the remittance of withheld taxes to the Bureau of Internal Revenue and to constantly monitor the monthly status of taxes withheld by the municipal government. Adhere strictly to the provisions of Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 of the BIR and observe the practice of intact remittance of the monthly outstanding amount of Due to BIR.

We recommended that the OIC City Treasurer require the collector to settle the unremitted collections of ₱58,200.00, produce the remaining missing AF 51 numbered 8713065-8713066, 8713068-8713093 and 8713095-8713100, and to investigate the deficiency noted and relieve the concerned collector from handling collecting function. Henceforth, require the revenue collectors to remit their collections daily and perform and strictly observe Section 30 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs, Volume I, to avoid the incurrence of the same deficiency. Moreover, the OIC City Treasurer must refrain from releasing subsequent Accountable Forms to the collectors until the previous Accountable Forms issued to them are reported.
observations.managementAction: No updated report yet from the Treasury Office

(b) The  Accountant to use the Certified List of Taxpayers prepared by the Office of the City Treasurer as the basis in recording the RPT/SEF tax receivables at the beginning of the year.

The City Assessor’s Office is still in the process of updating the RPTAR and evaluation of the revised assess value of Real Property.

The Assessor’s Office Conducted

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the (a) Local Chief Executive issue a written order to the members of the inventory committee, indicating therein the specific period for which the subject undertaking must be completed and the period of submission of the updated physical inventory report in accordance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual. Sanctions for the continued failure of the committee to come up with the required reports should also be expressly provided in the said order to prevent the recurrence of similar lapses in the future; (b) Reconcile the result of the actual physical inventory report with the accounting record. Follow the procedures listed in Section 491 of GAAM Volume I. For facility, we recommended that all offices start the count at their respective units and the GSO to consolidate the result; and (c) Instruct the head of the CGSO  to update and maintain the required records,  reports  and issue the necessary Property Acknowledgement Receipt / Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (PAR/ARE) for those issued PPEs under the custody of the end users as required under the regulation. And to facilitate such undertaking, assign additional competent personnel to Accounting and GSO in order to complete the maintenance and updating of PPELC and PC records of the respective offices.
observations.managementAction:
With verbal instruction to GSO to facilitate the physical inventory taking

The GSO/Inventory Committee was not able to complete the physical inventory

Instruction was issued to the GSO to address the noted deficiencies

There is a need for competent personnel to be assigned at GSO
observations.reason:
No proof that a written order from the Local Chief Executive was issued to GSO/ Inventory Committee

In the absence of complete inventory taking, the reconciliation procedures could not be undertaken.

Lack of competent personnel hindered the updating of records and issuance of  complete ARE/PARE corresponding to Property/equipment issued to custodian/end users

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the City Accountant to (a) reclassify the current portion of the Long-Term Loans Payable to Loans Payable-Current for fair presentation of the balances of the said accounts in the Statement of Financial Position as at end of the reporting period; and (b) henceforth observe accrual of current liabilities in accordance with PPSAS I.
observations.managementAction:
The subject account was already reclassified to its proper account.

Complied in accordance with PPSAS I

observations.recommendation: We therefore recommended that the City, through the City Treasurer, cause the immediate return of the remaining balances of PDAF funds to the National Treasury.
observations.managementAction: Complied, though the actual return of the subject PDAF to the Bureau of Treasury was made in February 2017 only.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) BAC members and     the City Engineer to properly plan and schedule all procurement activities to pave way for their timely implementation and completion of the projects; and (b) City Planning and Development Officer and the City Engineer to regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions may be introduced at the earliest time for optimum attainment of the intent of the 20% Development Fund.

Political differences hindered the timely approval of budget for CY 2016

CPDO  monitors the progress of project implementation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the (a) City Budget Officer to (i) work for the transfer of funds utilized under the GF and 20% DF from the SEF ; (ii) to observe proper charging of expenses to related budgets; and (b) City School Board to (i) comply strictly with the provisions of Section 100 of RA No. 7160; (ii) ensure that the budgeted amount for each year on SEF projects is fully obligated/utilized for the efficient and timely delivery of targeted services and benefits to the constituents.

100% of the budget for SEF projects were fully utilized for CY 2016

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Solid Waste Management Board (CSWMB) (i) exert effort to expedite the establishment and completion of the Solid Waste Management Facility; (ii) involve closely the Barangay officials in the strict implementation on the existing environmental laws and intensify the information campaign on the “no segregation, no collection policy and the “no-littering policy” among the residents; and (iii) exercise strong political will to implement the environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment as mandated under Republic Act  No. 9003.
